text.tspan: Telegram Mini App / Next.js product slice: companion-first UI,
privacy boundary, local guest start, Supabase persistence.
1. Dog context first. 2. No raw Telegram ID in UI. 3. Local mode must be useful. 4. Private data behind owner/auth
boundary. 5. Assistant is safety-bounded and context-aware.
Service map by product domain. Current implementation is mostly
app/page.tsx + route handlers; this board shows target ownership
Telegram session, email auth, profile, owner boundary
Pet core, passport, social behavior, public card source
Reminders, due actions, snooze/done/delete, daily return
Context-aware care/training answers with no diagnosis
Public card, print/PDF version, Telegram share fallback
Current schema: owner-scoped private data; public sharing is
query-backed today and should move to DB-backed public-safe cards.
Useful for redesign: every screen must justify its blocks by user
create local pet identity; no dead end
one next action, reminders, PDF/share shortcuts
approximate zones, privacy text, no exact public
answers from dog context, no diagnoses
care/gear list, future commerce hidden until
check-ux-surface-contract blocks technical statuses/internal service labels from returning to user screens.
